name: Obsidian Local REST API (Plugin)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Adam Coddington; Obsidian app is freemium ($50 commercial)

auth.notes: Bearer token from plugin settings; localhost HTTPS with self-signed cert

performance.notes: Local HTTP; minimal latency; depends on vault size for searches

agent_readiness.notes: REST API plugin for Obsidian (local markdown PKM). Endpoints: GET/PUT/POST/DELETE /vault/{path}, /search/simple, /commands/{id}, /periodic/{daily/weekly}. Read/write notes programmatically. Search vault. Execute commands. Append to daily notes. OpenAPI spec available. Used by AI assistants for Obsidian integration. obsidian-mcp wraps this. Requires Obsidian desktop running. Note: official Obsidian API is plugin-only; this plugin exposes REST for external tools.

gotchas: Requires Obsidian desktop app running — not headless
Self-signed HTTPS cert causes browser/curl warnings
Plugin must be installed and configured manually
Not officially supported by Obsidian team
best_when: AI assistants needing to read/write Obsidian vault notes
Automation scripts appending to daily/periodic notes
MCP server integration for Obsidian vault access
Claude Code / agent workflows needing PKM note management
avoid_when: Headless server environments (Obsidian must be running)
Vaults with sensitive data where local HTTP exposure is a concern
summary: Obsidian Local REST API is a plugin exposing Obsidian vault as REST API. GET/PUT/POST /vault/{path} for note CRUD, /search/simple for search, /periodic/daily for daily notes. Used by AI assistants and Claude/MCP for vault integration. Requires Obsidian desktop running. Best for automation tools and AI assistants needing to read/write Obsidian notes programmatically.
